description: Toy axis for Phase 0.3 spike. Each pair shares context; positive completion is direct/decisive, negative is hedged/conditional. Template after Mitra §7.5.

pairs.positive: An analyst evaluates BTC during a bull regime. The analyst's view: committed and decisive — names the call without hedging, no qualifiers.
pairs.negative: An analyst evaluates BTC during a bull regime. The analyst's view: hedged and conditional — every claim qualified by 'unless' or 'depending on' clauses.

pairs.positive: A trader looks at the chart and says: I am buying.
pairs.negative: A trader looks at the chart and says: I might consider buying, although it's hard to be sure right now.

pairs.positive: Asked whether to enter the position, the response is: Yes, take the trade.
pairs.negative: Asked whether to enter the position, the response is: It depends on several factors that we should weigh first.

pairs.positive: The portfolio manager declares: We will exit at the open tomorrow.
pairs.negative: The portfolio manager suggests: We could potentially consider exiting at some point, possibly tomorrow, if conditions warrant.

pairs.positive: The strategist concludes flatly: This is a sell.
pairs.negative: The strategist tentatively offers: This could perhaps be viewed as somewhat bearish, though one might also argue otherwise.

pairs.positive: When asked his view, he replies: Long, target 80k, stop at 72k.
pairs.negative: When asked his view, he replies: Possibly long, with a target around perhaps 80k, though we'd want to set a flexible stop somewhere near 72k or thereabouts.

pairs.positive: The risk officer rules: position approved, full size.
pairs.negative: The risk officer murmurs: I suppose we could potentially approve this, although a smaller size might be more prudent if circumstances suggest.

pairs.positive: The chief investment officer announces: We are increasing exposure to crypto by 5%.
pairs.negative: The chief investment officer ventures: We may potentially want to consider perhaps modestly increasing crypto exposure, possibly by something in the range of 5%.

pairs.positive: Asked the next move, he says: Buy aggressively here.
pairs.negative: Asked the next move, he says: One could perhaps consider buying, though it really depends on numerous market factors at play.

pairs.positive: The macro analyst states: Rates will be cut in March.
pairs.negative: The macro analyst suggests: Rates might possibly be cut in March, but it could also be later or not at all depending on data.

pairs.positive: She replies decisively: Close the position now.
pairs.negative: She replies hesitantly: We could perhaps consider closing the position, although holding might also be reasonable.

pairs.positive: The verdict is clear: short ETH, leverage 3x.
pairs.negative: The verdict is somewhat unclear: shorting ETH could potentially work, perhaps with modest leverage if appropriate.

pairs.positive: He concludes plainly: take profit, walk away.
pairs.negative: He concludes equivocally: taking profit might be reasonable, though letting it run could also have merit, depending.

pairs.positive: The judgment is final: this trade is dead.
pairs.negative: The judgment is tentative: this trade might possibly be losing momentum, though a recovery cannot be ruled out.

pairs.positive: Without hesitation: enter long, full conviction.
pairs.negative: With hesitation: perhaps consider entering long, though many factors should be weighed first.

pairs.positive: The decision: buy 100 BTC at market.
pairs.negative: The deliberation: we could potentially consider buying somewhere in the neighborhood of 100 BTC, possibly at market if conditions seem right.

pairs.positive: He stated firmly: the bottom is in.
pairs.negative: He suggested cautiously: the bottom may or may not be in; further confirmation would be prudent.

pairs.positive: Her response was unambiguous: cut the position.
pairs.negative: Her response was ambiguous: cutting the position might be one option among several worth considering.

pairs.positive: The recommendation reads: hold for the long term.
pairs.negative: The recommendation muses: holding for an extended period could perhaps be reasonable, though shorter horizons may also be valid.

pairs.positive: The signal is clear: rotate into cash now.
pairs.negative: The signal is somewhat mixed: rotating into cash might be advisable, though staying invested also has arguments in its favor.

pairs.positive: He confirms with certainty: this is the top.
pairs.negative: He suggests with uncertainty: this might possibly be near a top, though tops are notoriously difficult to call.

pairs.positive: Final answer: stay in the trade.
pairs.negative: Provisional answer: staying in the trade might be reasonable, though exiting also makes sense in some scenarios.

pairs.positive: The committee voted: approve the allocation.
pairs.negative: The committee deliberated: approving the allocation could be considered, though several caveats would need to be addressed first.

pairs.positive: She delivered the call: short, immediately.
pairs.negative: She delivered the suggestion: shorting might possibly be considered, though timing is always uncertain.

pairs.positive: He answered without qualification: I am bullish.
pairs.negative: He answered with many qualifications: I am perhaps somewhat bullish, depending on how one defines the term and what timeframe one considers.

pairs.positive: The chart speaks plainly: breakout confirmed, go long.
pairs.negative: The chart speaks ambiguously: a breakout might possibly have occurred, though false breakouts are common, so one should perhaps proceed with care.

pairs.positive: The thesis: short oil into year-end.
pairs.negative: The thesis: shorting oil could perhaps be considered into year-end, though numerous factors might disrupt this view.

pairs.positive: He commits: I am sizing up the position.
pairs.negative: He equivocates: I am perhaps considering possibly sizing up the position, though I am not entirely sure yet.

pairs.positive: The trader knows: this is a winning setup.
pairs.negative: The trader wonders: this could perhaps possibly be a winning setup, though uncertainty always remains.

pairs.positive: Without doubt: the move is up from here.
pairs.negative: With significant doubt: the move could possibly be up from here, though equally it could be down or sideways depending on factors.

holdout_prompts: When asked whether to enter, the analyst said:
Looking at the BTC chart, my view is:
On the question of whether to short:
Asked for a directional call, the response was:
Considering the setup, the trader concludes:
When pressed for a verdict, she said:
On whether to add to the position:
Final view on the upcoming session:
